perturbation.original_text: I will keep everything I see, touch or hear strictly confidential. In particular, I agree NOT, to blog or otherwise post any Confidential Motorola Information to any social media websites.
perturbation.changed_text: I will keep everything I see, touch or hear reasonably secure. In particular, I agree NOT, to blog or otherwise post any Confidential Motorola Information to any social media websites.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'strictly confidential' is changed to 'reasonably secure,' introducing a subjective standard. This weakens the confidentiality obligation and creates uncertainty as to what level of security is required, leading to a contradiction in the enforceability of the confidentiality clause. The term 'reasonably secure' introduces a subjective element that is difficult to define.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I will keep everything I see, touch or hear reasonably secure. In particular, I agree NOT, to blog or otherwise post any Confidential Motorola Information to any social media websites.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'strictly confidential,' establishing a clear standard. The changed text replaces this with 'reasonably secure,' introducing a subjective and vague standard. This change directly contradicts the need for a strict confidentiality obligation because 'reasonably secure' is open to interpretation and weaker enforcement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text but does not resolve the contradiction; it merely restates the problem. Because the contract now contains this vague requirement, and based on the explanation provided, this constitutes a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
